items.title: Spam classification labels
items.scenario: An email filter assigns each message a label Y ∈ {0, 1} where 0 = ham and 1 = spam. After training, the model outputs predicted probabilities p̂(0) and p̂(1) that sum to 1—an estimated PMF over the label random variable Y.
items.explanation: Y is a discrete random variable with two values. The model's softmax (or sigmoid in binary form) produces a PMF; cross-entropy measures how far p̂(y) is from the true label distribution.

items.title: LLM next-token prediction
items.scenario: A language model sees context 'The capital of France is' and outputs a probability vector over ~50,000 vocabulary tokens. Token index T is a discrete random variable; the model's softmax layer defines a PMF p(t) for each possible next token t.
items.explanation: Sampling temperature scales logits before softmax, reshaping the PMF. Greedy decoding picks argmax_t p(t); nucleus sampling draws from a truncated PMF—both are operations on a discrete random variable's distribution.

items.title: Inference latency as a continuous RV
items.scenario: An MLOps team models API response time X (milliseconds) as a continuous random variable. They fit a log-normal PDF to historical latencies and compute P(X > 500) to estimate SLA breach rate.
items.explanation: Individual exact millisecond values have probability zero; only intervals have positive probability via PDF integration. The CDF F(500) = P(X ≤ 500) supports percentile dashboards (p95, p99).
